title: US Threatens Spain Over NATO
summary: European leaders met in Cyprus to discuss the EU budget, but a new problem came up. A leaked email from the US Pentagon said the US might punish Spain because Spain did not support the US-Israel war against Iran. The email suggested the US could try to kick Spain out of NATO. But NATO rules do not allow expelling a member. Other European leaders like the Dutch prime minister and a German official defended Spain. They said Spain is a full NATO member and should stay that way. The Italian prime minister also criticized the US for tensions with Spain. The US president, Donald Trump, is angry with Spain and other countries like the UK for not fully supporting the war. This is causing problems between the US and its European allies.

keywords.explanation: A group of countries that agree to help each other if one is attacked.
The building where the US military leaders work.
Secret information that gets out to the public.
To force someone to leave a group.
A country that helps another country, especially in war.
A meeting of leaders from different countries.

questions.question: Why did the US threaten Spain?
options: Spain did not support the US-Israel war against Iran
Spain refused to pay NATO dues
questions.correct_answer: Spain did not support the US-Israel war against Iran

questions.question: What did the leaked email suggest the US could do to Spain?
options: Try to kick Spain out of NATO
questions.correct_answer: Try to kick Spain out of NATO

questions.question: Can a country be expelled from NATO according to its rules?
options: No, there is no rule for that
Only if the US says so
questions.correct_answer: No, there is no rule for that

questions.question: What did the Dutch prime minister say about Spain?
options: Spain is a full NATO member
questions.correct_answer: Spain is a full NATO member

questions.question: Which other country is also in trouble with the US?
Why is the US president angry with the UK?
options: The UK did not allow use of bases for attacks on Iran at first
questions.correct_answer: The UK did not allow use of bases for attacks on Iran at first

background_read: NATO is a military alliance of 32 countries from North America and Europe. They promise to defend each other if one is attacked. The United States is a very powerful member. Spain is also a member. Sometimes, countries in NATO disagree about things like wars. But they usually try to work together.
Article_Structure: WHO: European leaders, including Spain's Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez, Dutch PM Rob Jetten, Italian PM Giorgia Meloni, and US President Donald Trump.
WHAT: A leaked US Pentagon email threatened to punish Spain for not supporting the US-Israel war against Iran, possibly by trying to expel Spain from NATO.
WHERE: European leaders were meeting in Cyprus, and the threat came from the US.
WHY: The US was angry that Spain did not support its military actions against Iran.

perspectives.description: Spain says it is fulfilling its NATO duties and should not be punished.
They defended Spain and said it is a full NATO member.
The US thinks allies should fully support its war efforts.
